options.text: The limit for man entry of personnel into tanks
The concentration of a hydrocarbon gas in air above which there is insufficient oxygen to support combustion
The limit where there is insufficient inert gas to support combustion
The limit where there is too much inert gas to support combustion

explanation: The Upper Flammable Limit (UFL) is the maximum concentration of a hydrocarbon gas in air above which the mixture is too 'rich' (insufficient oxygen) to support combustion.
